title: **Balance Sheet Overview – As of December 31, 2026**
content:
The most material balance-sheet development this period was the significant expansion in cash holdings, which rose by $45 million to reach a total asset value of $81.7 billion and contributed substantially to overall growth.

Investment securities increased slightly as they added to the earning-asset base outside of lending, while loans and leases held steady at their current level representing the core portfolio balance. Other assets grew notably by 17.4 percent adding non-lending liquidity reserves that complemented cash positions in managing short-term needs.

Loans and Leases remain the largest component on the asset side as they constitute over sixty-three percent of total holdings, providing a stable foundation for member lending activities while other categories adjusted to market conditions without altering this primary structure significantly.

Liabilities increased primarily through shares/deposits which grew by 3.9 percent reflecting continued member confidence and funding support that matched the expansion in asset growth across all major categories during this reporting period.

Equity rose substantially driven by undivided earnings accumulation, while accumulated unrealized gains declined due to market fluctuations affecting available-for-sale securities valuations; overall balance sheet movement reflects a strategic shift toward liquidity preservation through cash buildup funded largely by member deposits.
